CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT, str_value VARCHAR(255));
SELECT not_like(str_value, '_%b%', '#', false) AS match_result FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT not_like(str_value, '_%b%', '#', false) AS match_result FROM test_strings WHERE id IN (2, 3, 4);
SELECT id, str_value FROM test_strings WHERE not_like(str_value, '_%b%', '#', true);
SELECT t1.str_value FROM test_strings AS t1 JOIN test_strings AS t2 ON t1.id <> t2.id WHERE not_like(t1.str_value, '_%b%', '#', false);
CREATE TABLE patterns (id INT, pattern VARCHAR(50));
SELECT ts.id, ts.str_value FROM test_strings ts JOIN patterns p ON not_like(ts.str_value, p.pattern, '#', false) WHERE p.id = 1;
SELECT ts.id, ts.str_value FROM (SELECT * FROM test_strings WHERE id > 2) AS ts WHERE not_like(ts.str_value, 'a_%', '#', true);
SELECT CASE WHEN not_like('test_string', '_%g%', '#', false) THEN 'Not Matched' ELSE 'Matched' END AS match_status;
